name: TCP-like state machine
task: Create /tmp/eval_tcp/tcp.py implementing class TCPConnection with a simplified TCP state machine. States (string constants): CLOSED, LISTEN, SYN_SENT, SYN_RECEIVED, ESTABLISHED, FIN_WAIT, CLOSE_WAIT, CLOSED_FINAL. Initial state is CLOSED. Methods: listen(), connect(), syn_received(), syn_ack_received(), ack_received(), close(), fin_received(). Each method either advances state along the standard TCP path or raises InvalidTransition (an exception class you define) if the call is illegal in the current state. Active open: CLOSED -> connect() -> SYN_SENT -> syn_ack_received() -> ESTABLISHED -> close() -> FIN_WAIT -> ack_received() -> CLOSED_FINAL. Passive open: CLOSED -> listen() -> LISTEN -> syn_received() -> SYN_RECEIVED -> ack_received() -> ESTABLISHED -> fin_received() -> CLOSE_WAIT -> close() -> CLOSED_FINAL. The .state attribute exposes the current state.
